report:
## Growth

Both GOOGL and META delivered strong top-line growth through 2025, though META's acceleration into early 2026 appears particularly striking. META's total revenue reached roughly $200.97 billion for full-year 2025, with advertising revenue up 22% year-over-year and 'Other revenue' surging 50% [META:S1]; by Q1 2026, regional growth rates had accelerated sharply, with Europe up 39% and Rest of World up 40% versus the same period in 2025 [META:S3]. GOOGL also posted broad-based growth: U.S. revenues rose 13% and APAC revenues rose 18% on a constant-currency basis for the nine months ended September 30, 2025 [GOOGL:S2], while Google Cloud revenues increased $15.5 billion from 2024 to 2025, driven by infrastructure and platform services [GOOGL:S3]. GOOGL's EMEA growth of 14% as reported (12% constant currency) reflects some foreign-exchange headwind [GOOGL:S2], a dynamic META also flagged for full-year 2025 [META:S4]. Overall, both companies are compounding at double-digit rates, with META showing a steeper recent acceleration and GOOGL demonstrating diversified growth across Search, Cloud, and subscriptions.

**GOOGL:** GOOGL sustained double-digit revenue growth across all major geographies through mid-2025, with U.S. revenues up 13% and APAC up 18% on a constant-currency basis for the nine months ended September 30, 2025 [GOOGL:S2]. Google Cloud was a standout driver, adding $15.5 billion in revenue from 2024 to 2025 on the back of infrastructure and platform services demand [GOOGL:S3], while YouTube and Google One subscription growth also contributed meaningfully [GOOGL:S3]. Foreign currency movements, particularly U.S. dollar strength, created modest headwinds in EMEA and Other Americas [GOOGL:S2].

**META:** META posted full-year 2025 total revenue of approximately $200.97 billion, with Family of Apps advertising revenue growing 22% and Other revenue growing 50% year-over-year [META:S1]. Growth momentum appears to have accelerated into early 2026, with Q1 2026 regional revenue increases of 29–40% across all geographies versus Q1 2025 [META:S3], supported by AI-driven ad improvements and an 11% year-over-year increase in engineering headcount [META:S0]. Foreign exchange was a modest headwind for full-year 2025 [META:S4], but underlying demand trends remain robust.

---

quality.notes: Well-structured report with clear parallel treatment of both companies. Citations are consistently applied and specific. The comparison is genuinely analytical rather than merely descriptive, noting acceleration differences and FX dynamics for both. Minor concern on honesty dimension: the report presents META Q1 2026 figures alongside GOOGL data only through mid-2025 (nine months ended Sept 30, 2025), creating an asymmetric time horizon that is not explicitly flagged as a limitation—this could mislead readers into thinking the acceleration comparison is apples-to-apples. A brief caveat acknowledging the different reporting periods would improve transparency.
